summary: The runner consolidation homed the spawn but left the github finding-line extraction copied five times — and bundle.rs regrew `check_harness` verbatim, one commit after it was consolidated

edit.description: Home the extraction on the value that owns the bytes: add a `findings()` method to `CheckRun` (149) returning the github-reporter finding lines — `output.lines().filter(|l| l.starts_with("::")).map(str::to_string).collect()`, the body now spelled at 187-192 inside `check_harness` (181). `check_harness` keeps its `(Vec<String>, bool)` shape and composes the method rather than open-coding the filter, exactly as it already composes `check_in` for the spawn. `CheckRun` is the right home over a free function: the filter is a projection of `output`, and every one of the five copies below already holds a `CheckRun` (or an expression producing one) at the point it re-spells the filter. `check_in`'s doc (155-161) already claims the spawn home; extend the same claim to the finding shape so the doc's narrowness cannot license the next regrowth — the root cause a9a21a9 named for the six `check_harness` copies.

236-241 is `check_harness(&out)` re-spelled verbatim, regrown one commit after CHECK-HARNESS-ONE-HOME (3bd0f80) consolidated six copies of exactly this: `common::check_in(&out, &["--harness", out.to_str().unwrap()], Some("github"))` then the `::` filter is byte-for-byte the home's own body (`tests/common/mod.rs:181-194`), and the assertions below want both halves it returns (`run.ok` at 245, `findings` at 247). Collapse to `let (findings, ok) = common::check_harness(&out);` — the file already `mod common`s and calls `common::check_in`. This is the sixth copy, not a fifth: it duplicates the composed home, not just the filter.

Two copies. The local `fn check_in(root, args) -> (Vec<String>, bool)` (29-38) re-spells the filter at 34 — fold its body to `let run = common::check_in(root, args, Some("github")); (run.findings(), run.ok)`. Keep the local wrapper itself: its `["."]`-arg shape is not `check_harness`'s `--harness` spelling, so it is a composition of the home, not a rival to it — the shape `cli.rs`'s `run_check_harness` (95) already establishes as sanctioned. Its name shadowing `common::check_in` with a different signature is pre-existing and NOT this entry's to rename — say so rather than folding a rename in silently. Second copy: 177-180 open-codes the filter inline (as `Vec<&str>`, the one `&str` spelling) purely to assert emptiness — becomes `run.findings()`, whose `Vec<String>` serves `is_empty()` and the `{findings:#?}` message identically.

`check_two_step` (77-82) re-spells the filter at 80 over `common::check_in(harness, &["."], Some("github"))`. Becomes `common::check_in(harness, &["."], Some("github")).findings()`. Its `["."]` args are deliberate and documented (70-76: the path argument resolves as a harness root so `check` reads members live off harness disk) — that spelling survives untouched; only the filter folds. The file's other seven runs already reach `common::check_harness` (131, 161, 179, 212, 228, 267, 285) and are not this entry's to touch.

tests.asserts: unchanged behavior — the suites ARE the test: every folded caller keeps its current assertions green, since the extraction is pure over `CheckRun.output`

acceptance: `rg 'starts_with\("::"\)' tests/` finds exactly one home — `CheckRun::findings` in `tests/common/mod.rs` — and `check_harness` composes it rather than open-coding it. No `check_in(root, ["--harness", root], Some("github"))`-plus-filter spelling survives beside `check_harness`. `cargo test` and `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green.
notes: scoped at 8036900. Third consolidation over this scaffolding, and the pattern is now legible: 3bd0f80 homed the harness shape, a9a21a9 homed the spawn, each leaving the layer below it copied. This entry takes the layer a9a21a9 left. Disjoint from both parks (src/graph.rs, release.yml). The five filter copies and bundle.rs's regrown `check_harness` were all re-read on disk at 8036900.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. All cites re-verified unmoved at 8036900. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (65) sits under a doc (61-64) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (627) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (654), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 525 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 624 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 649-652 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1356, re-verified unmoved at 8036900) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1357-1361) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1359) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 8036900 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; inbox empty and the window is one test-scaffolding commit touching no import mechanics: `git diff 3593ab6..8036900 -- src/graph.rs` is empty. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` still reads 5 at 65 under a cite claiming five. Stays parked: the constant may still be wrong. rescoped 4a33911; re-cited 53519b9; re-verified 8036900.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 8036900, holding on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. The reconciled window is tests-only and touched no release surface. scoped at b676656.
